ai_strategy_lead.name: Leonum Institute for AI and Emerging Technologies

ai_strategy.summary: Catholic University of America has a public AI posture anchored in Leonum Institute for AI and Emerging Technologies. The cited sources show activity across responsible-use guidance, teaching resources, research or infrastructure, and AI literacy or workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use AI transparently and follow course, research, workplace and academic-integrity expectations.
Do not enter confidential, regulated, sensitive, unpublished or protected institutional data into unapproved public AI tools.
Human users remain accountable for accuracy, bias, copyright, privacy and final decisions based on AI output.
ai_policy.summary: Catholic University of America publishes AI policy or guidance with a permissive stance: AI use is allowed or actively explored, but bounded by privacy, ethics, academic integrity, compliance and human-review expectations.

governance_structure.summary: AI governance at Catholic University of America is evidenced through Leonum Institute for AI and Emerging Technologies and the cited policy or strategy sources. Public sources did not identify a separate chief AI officer during this sweep.

initiatives.title: Google Gemini access for university Google Workspace accounts
initiatives.description: Catholic University lists Gemini as an AI assistant available through university Google Workspace accounts, adding a named enterprise AI platform beyond general classroom guidance.
initiatives.significance: The listing turns a generic platform gap into a concrete institutional AI-tool deployment signal.

investment.description: Public support page does not disclose contract value, active-user count, or license cost.

collaborations.description: Gemini is provided through the university Google Workspace environment.

initiatives.policy_implications: Extends institution-supported AI access while remaining under university privacy and use expectations.

regulatory_context.summary: No binding state higher-education AI policy was identified during this sweep; institutional guidance is the main policy evidence.

narrative_summary: Catholic University of America has a documented AI posture across policy, teaching, research, infrastructure or access. The central public signal is Leonum Institute for AI and Emerging Technologies, supported by policy or guidance sources that encourage responsible use while emphasizing privacy, academic integrity, ethics and human oversight. The institution also shows curriculum or AI literacy activity and at least one research, center, infrastructure or applied innovation signal. Its maturity is strongest where public sources show durable structures: committees, centers, portals, degree programs, supercomputing, enterprise tools, or workforce initiatives. The gaps are also important: this sweep did not find a standalone chief AI officer in public sources for most assigned institutions, and several initiatives do not disclose exact budgets, user counts or implementation metrics. Overall, the evidence supports a medium-confidence R1 profile with enough public signals to compare the institution across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tools, workforce and equity dimensions.

gaps_acknowledged: Public sources did not identify a standalone chief AI officer role during this sweep.
Some AI initiatives did not disclose dollar amounts, user counts, or detailed implementation metrics.
Leadership names were not populated because the assigned task prioritized missing AI posture profiles and every non-obvious AI claim is URL-cited.
Catholic University now has a named Gemini Workspace support page and IgniteHub AI-portal student-support case, but public sources still do not disclose active-user counts or contract cost.
No public GPU count or named university supercomputer was found for Catholic University beyond AI laboratory activity.
